mission: Convert the 35 ported `.opencode/tool/` plugin-style tools into built-in Effect `Tool.define` modules in `packages/opencode/src/tool/`, register them in the tool registry, and wire the DuckDB analytical backend for storage-backed tools.

duckdb_pipeline.status: published_candidate_boundary — fully implemented but not yet checkpointed into git
modified_files: packages/opencode/src/storage/db.duckdb.ts (added connection pool, CLI client, layer with background pipeline init)
dirty_preserved: packages/opencode/src/storage/adapter.ts (pre-existing dirty — not modified by pipeline)

key_files_summary.pipeline.ts: Exports runPipeline(), layer, defaultLayer. Uses duckdb subprocess (NO -readonly flag, this is the WRITE path). Idempotent via _pipeline_meta table. Runs in background fiber to avoid blocking startup.
key_files_summary.duckdb-config.ts: Reads OPENCODE_DUCKDB_PATH env var (optional). Provides Service tag via ConfigService pattern.
key_files_summary.schema.duckdb.ts: Defines 2 analytical views (SESSION_CLUSTERING_VIEW, TOOL_USAGE_VIEW) via initViewsSql().
key_files_summary.db.duckdb.ts: Connection pool (max 4 concurrent subprocesses). CLI client spawns duckdb WITH -readonly flag (READ path). Also provides DuckDBAdapter for Drizzle compatibility.
key_files_summary.duckdb-firewall.ts: Blocks read_text, read_blob, read_csv, read_parquet, read_json, ATTACH, LOAD, EXPORT for agent-facing queries.

duckdb_write_path.description: The pipeline.ts spawns duckdb WITHOUT -readonly — this is the only write-capable connection. Tools that need to write to DuckDB (wave records, findings, diagnostics) must go through a dedicated write client, NOT DuckDB.Service (which is -readonly).
duckdb_write_path.current_architecture: runPipeline() in pipeline.ts spawns duckdb <path> -json -c <sql> — no -readonly. DuckDB.Service spawns duckdb <path> -readonly -json -c <sql>.
duckdb_write_path.implication: New analytical tables (_analytics_waves, _analytics_findings, _analytics_registry, _analytics_diagnostics) must be created by pipeline.ts during its init phase, since the pipeline has write access. The DuckDB.Service cannot CREATE them.

tool_port.status: 35 plugin-style tools placed in .opencode/tool/ by user. Each uses @opencode-ai/plugin API (tool.schema, spawnSync python3 embedded scripts).

tool_patterns_observed.common_imports: import { tool } from "@opencode-ai/plugin"; import { spawnSync } from "node:child_process"; import { resolve } from "node:path"
tool_patterns_observed.common_helpers: resolvePath(worktree, p) — resolves relative paths; summarize(output) — trims whitespace; runPython(worktree, script, ...args) — spawnSync("python3", ["-", ...args], ...) with embedded Python script string
tool_patterns_observed.common_structure: export default tool({ description, args, async execute(args, context) { ... } })
tool_patterns_observed.execute_pattern: Call runPython() with embedded Python script → parse stdout/stderr → return formatted result string
tool_patterns_observed.context_usage: context.worktree — base path for resolving file paths; context.sessionID — unique session identifier; context.agent — calling agent name

core_architectural_decision.title: Dual-Backend Storage Pattern for Analytical Tools
core_architectural_decision.pattern: Each tool that produces or queries structured analytical data should support two backends: DuckDB (when configured) and JSONL (fallback).
core_architectural_decision.rule: When OPENCODE_DUCKDB_PATH is configured AND DuckDB.Service is available → use DuckDB for storage/querying. Otherwise → fall back to JSONL files (current behavior). This mirrors how the DuckDB pipeline works (only runs when config is set).
core_architectural_decision.write_path_note: Tools that WRITE to DuckDB must NOT use DuckDB.Service (it spawns with -readonly). Instead, they use a dedicated write client spawned without -readonly. Tools that READ from DuckDB CAN use DuckDB.Service.
core_architectural_decision.dualism_justification: DuckDB provides fast SQL analytics across sessions. JSONL provides simple, inspectable, version-control-friendly persistence with zero dependencies. Supporting both ensures the repo works identically with or without DuckDB installed.

file_only_no_duckdb.description: These tools write to version-controlled JSON files or are coordination/file-ops. No DuckDB integration needed.
tools: propose_plan — writes docs/json/opencode/plans/*.v1.json
revise_plan — revises existing plan artifact (same location)
comment_plan — appends critic comments to plan JSONL
review_criticism — reads plan + critic comments from same location
send_message — writes to .rig/messages/*.jsonl, real-time cross-session coordination
read_messages — reads from .rig/messages/*.jsonl
prepare_delegation — coordination intent recording
prepare_checkpoint — git operations intent
publish_checkpoint — git operations intent + storage
generate_published_checkpoint_report — git operations report
inspect_failure — message inspection, no persistence
prepublication_admitted — writes to docs/
prepublication_blocked — writes to docs/
prepublication_inconclusive — writes to docs/
curate_context — session-local context assembly, too transient for DuckDB
read_artifact — reads from JSON files, could be enhanced with DuckDB for queries
read_source — reads source code, not analytical data
read_lib — reads framework internals, not analytical data
smart_edit — file editing operation, not analytical
smart_write — file writing operation, not analytical
smart_batch — multi-file editing, not analytical
replace_symbol — code transformation, not analytical

duckdb_aware_dual_backend.description: These tools produce or query structured analytical data. They should write to DuckDB (when available) AND always write to JSONL as canonical persistence. Reads can use DuckDB.Service.
tools: record_stress_wave → write to DuckDB _analytics_waves table + docs/json/opencode/sessions/*/waves/stress.v1.jsonl
record_execution_wave → write to DuckDB _analytics_waves table + docs/json/opencode/sessions/*/waves/execution.v1.jsonl
qa_observed_clean → write to DuckDB _analytics_qa_observations table + JSONL
out_of_scope_finding → write to DuckDB _analytics_findings table + docs/findings/out-of-scope-findings.jsonl + cross-session registry
publish_finding → write to DuckDB _analytics_registry table + docs/json/opencode/registry/artifacts.v1.jsonl
discover_findings → query from DuckDB _analytics_registry OR JSONL, with TTL filtering
tool_failure → write to DuckDB _analytics_diagnostics table + JSONL
tool_feedback → write to DuckDB _analytics_diagnostics table + JSONL
generate_report → could use DuckDB for session summary (low priority)
rig_jsonl_query → query JSONL artifacts via DuckDB subprocess (bypass firewall for read_json_auto). Uses `uv run --with duckdb python3 -` pattern or DuckDB.Service when available.
rig_schema_validate → schema validation, independent of DuckDB but could use for schema registry queries

already_builtin_merge_from_opencode.description: These tools already exist as built-in Effect Tool.define modules. Compare the .opencode/ version (which may have Python-script logic, circular buffers, heartbeat tracking) and port any additional functionality into the built-in version.

pairs.differences: Built-in version is simpler (no dry-run, no heartbeat check, no circular buffer). .opencode version has Python script with: heartbeat tracking, 10-second activity check, circular buffer (last 5 heartbeats + last 5 messages combined), dry-run mode, formatted output with ASCII art.
pairs.merge_recommendation: Port dry-run support and formatted output from .opencode version. Heartbeat and circular buffer are coordination-layer concerns — defer if they add too much complexity.

pairs.differences: Very similar — both do JSONL file reading with type filtering and limit. .opencode version may have additional filtering logic (unread-only mode, since/until time filters). File needs inspection.
pairs.merge_recommendation: Inspect .opencode version for unread-only and time-based filters; port if present.

pairs.differences: Built-in version writes to Storage.Service (Effect-based). .opencode version probably has Python validation logic. File needs inspection.
pairs.merge_recommendation: Inspect for additional validation logic not present in built-in.

pairs.differences: Both exist. Inspect .opencode version for additional reporting features.
pairs.merge_recommendation: Inspect and port any missing logic.

pairs.differences: Built-in version is simple (creates CheckpointRecord via checkpoint-tools helper). .opencode version may have Python validation.
Built-in version uses failure-tools helper. .opencode version may have Python analysis logic.

remain_as_plugins.reason: GitHub-specific tools that use @opencode-ai/plugin API as intended. No need to convert to built-in.

duckdb_schema_extensions_needed.description: The pipeline currently creates _pipeline_meta, _pipeline_session, and _pipeline_part tables. The new analytical tools need additional tables. These must be added to schema.duckdb.ts and created by pipeline.ts (since pipeline has write access).

sql_definitions._analytics_waves:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _analytics_waves (
  session_id TEXT, plan_id TEXT, wave_type TEXT, adversary TEXT,
  attack_surface TEXT, attacks_attempted TEXT, findings TEXT,
  verdict TEXT, recorded_at BIGINT
)
sql_definitions._analytics_findings:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _analytics_findings (
  finding_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  session_id TEXT, affected_files TEXT, language TEXT,
  why_matters TEXT, best_practice_anchor TEXT, recommended_slice TEXT,
  finding_type TEXT, confidence REAL, ttl_seconds BIGINT,
  expires_at BIGINT, recorded_at BIGINT
)
sql_definitions._analytics_registry:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _analytics_registry (
  dedup_key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  finding_id TEXT, session_id TEXT, finding_type TEXT,
  summary TEXT, source_artifact TEXT, relevance_profiles TEXT,
  confidence REAL, ttl_seconds BIGINT, published_at BIGINT,
  expires_at BIGINT
)
sql_definitions._analytics_qa_observations:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _analytics_qa_observations (
  session_id TEXT, plan_id TEXT, boundary TEXT,
  tests_examined TEXT, production_paths_exercised TEXT,
  notes TEXT, recorded_at BIGINT
)
sql_definitions._analytics_diagnostics:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _analytics_diagnostics (
  session_id TEXT, tool_name TEXT, error_message TEXT,
  args_used TEXT, recovery_attempted INTEGER, recorded_at BIGINT
)

files_to_modify: packages/opencode/src/storage/schema.duckdb.ts — add the CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS SQL to a new initTablesSql() function or append to the view init
packages/opencode/src/storage/pipeline.ts — call initTablesSql() during pipeline init (before or alongside initViewsSql())
duckdb_schema_extensions_needed.recommended_approach: Add a new function initWriteTablesSql() in schema.duckdb.ts that returns the combined CREATE TABLE SQL. Call it from runPipeline() in pipeline.ts after creating the _pipeline_meta table. The analytical views can then reference these tables.

tool_implementation_pattern.from_plugin_api: import { tool } from "@opencode-ai/plugin"; export default tool({ description, args, async execute }) — wraps embedded Python scripts via spawnSync('python3', ['-', ...])
tool_implementation_pattern.to_builtin_api: import { Effect, Schema } from "effect"; import * as Tool from "./tool"; export const XTool = Tool.define("x", Effect.gen(function* () { return { description, parameters: Schema.Struct({...}), execute: (params) => Effect.gen(function* () { ... }).pipe(Effect.orDie) } }))
key_transformations: Replace async execute() with Effect.gen execute()
Replace spawnSync(python3) embedded scripts with direct TypeScript/Effect operations using: AppFileSystem.Service for file I/O, Effect JSON parsing, Date.now() for timestamps
Replace context.worktree with InstanceState.context.directory (Effect-based worktree resolution)
Replace context.sessionID with ctx.sessionID (same property, available in Tool.Context)
Replace context.agent with ctx.agent (same property, available in Tool.Context)
Export via export const XTool = Tool.define(...) followed by export * as X from './x'
Always use Effect.orDie at the end of execute to convert unexpected errors into defects
Use Effect.tryPromise for JSON.parse() and other effectful sync operations

dual_backend_pattern_duckdb_tools.description: Each DuckDB-aware tool follows this pattern:

duckdb_write_client_approach.problem: DuckDB.Service is read-only (spawns with -readonly flag). Tools need a write-capable client to INSERT data into analytical tables.

options.option: A. Dedicated write client per tool
options.description: Each tool spawns its own duckdb subprocess WITHOUT -readonly for writes. Simple but spawns many processes.
options.cons: Multiple subprocess spawns per tool call, no connection reuse

options.description: Create a DuckDBWritePool.Service (like DuckDB.Service but without -readonly) that tools can yield.
options.pros: Connection reuse, limits concurrent writes to 1 (DuckDB single-writer constraint)
options.cons: New service to create and wire into layer chain

options.option: C. Write through the pipeline's background fiber
options.description: Queue writes via a channel that the pipeline fiber processes sequentially.

duckdb_write_client_approach.recommendation: Option B is preferred — create a lightweight DuckDBWritePool service in a new file or add a non-readonly client to db.duckdb.ts. The pipeline.ts already has the no-readonly spawn pattern to reuse.

files_to_create.naming_convention: kebab-case (.ts extension, same name as the .opencode/ underscore version but with hyphens)
files_to_create.notes: The user stated 28, but inventory confirms 27 new tools after accounting for 2 GitHub plugins and 6 already-existing built-ins. The possible off-by-one is the prepare_delegation tool (listed in the 28 but counted differently).

registry_registration.action: Add imports for all 27 new tools + update 6 existing registrations. The existing registry has a pre-existing dirty state from other lanes — all edits must be additive.
registry_registration.existing_dirty_patterns: Lines 261-266 have indentation inconsistencies (tab stop vs spaces for the coordination tool block). New tool registrations should follow the same style as the existing block.
registry_registration.layer_provisioning: New tools that need AppFileSystem, DuckDB, or other services will get them automatically from the registry's defaultLayer chain. No additional layer provisioning needed unless they require a new service.

duckdb_schema.action: Add initWriteTablesSql() function that returns CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS SQL for all 5 analytical tables (_analytics_waves, _analytics_findings, _analytics_registry, _analytics_qa_observations, _analytics_diagnostics).

duckdb_pipeline.action: Import initWriteTablesSql() from schema.duckdb.ts and call it during runPipeline() after creating _pipeline_meta but before or alongside initViewsSql().
duckdb_pipeline.exact_location:
After line 67 (after CREATE_PART_TABLE execution) and before line 70 (before initViewsSql()):
```ts
yield* Effect.promise(() => execDuckDB(dbPath, initWriteTablesSql(), signal))
```

opencode_version_has: Dry-run mode (dry_run parameter, preview output without writing)
Heartbeat tracking (reads existing messages, separates heartbeats from messages)
10-second recipient heartbeat check (fails if recipient not active)
Circular buffer (keeps last 5 heartbeats + last 5 messages)
Kind validation (directive, blocker, clarification, handoff, wave_start, wave_complete)
send_message_compare.merge_recommendation: Port dry_run, kind, wave, subject, body fields to the built-in Schema. Port formatted output. Defer heartbeat/circular buffer for now (requires cross-session coordination layer that doesn't exist yet in the Effect runtime).

read_messages_compare.opencode_version_has: Unread-only mode, time-based filters (since/until). File needs inspection to confirm.
read_messages_compare.builtin_version_has: Effect-based, Schema.Struct parameters, AFS file reading, type filter, limit enforcement (1-200).
read_messages_compare.merge_recommendation: Port any missing filter fields (unread_only, since, until) after inspecting .opencode version.

steps.title: Checkpoint DuckDB pipeline
steps.description: Commit current DuckDB pipeline state (pipeline.ts, duckdb-config.ts, schema.duckdb.ts, db.duckdb.ts, registry.ts) to git so the next session starts from a clean baseline.

steps.title: Extend DuckDB schema — add analytical tables
steps.description: Add initWriteTablesSql() to schema.duckdb.ts for all 5 analytical tables. Call it from pipeline.ts after _pipeline_meta creation. Run typecheck to verify.

steps.title: Merge 6 existing built-ins
steps.description: Compare each .opencode/ version with its built-in counterpart. Port additional logic (dry-run support, kind/validation, formatted output) from .opencode/ versions into the built-in. Do NOT delete the .opencode/ files.

steps.title: Create simplest tools first
steps.description: tool-failure.ts, tool-feedback.ts, qa-observed-clean.ts. These have simple JSONL + DuckDB write patterns. Straightforward to implement — good warm-up.

steps.title: Create wave recording tools
steps.description: record-stress-wave.ts, record-execution-wave.ts. Same pattern as step 4 (JSONL + DuckDB write). Build on the write helpers established in step 4.

steps.title: Create finding tools
steps.description: out-of-scope-finding.ts, publish-finding.ts, discover-findings.ts. Share cross-session registry pattern. out-of-scope-finding is complex — it writes to two locations (findings JSONL + registry JSONL) and does SHA-256 dedup. The Python script logic needs careful translation.

steps.title: Create plan pipeline tools
steps.description: propose-plan.ts, revise-plan.ts, comment-plan.ts, review-criticism.ts. File-based (no DuckDB). Write to docs/json/opencode/plans/*.json. Share code patterns (JSON file reading/writing, validation, error formatting).

steps.title: Create prepublication tools
steps.description: prepublication-admitted.ts, prepublication-blocked.ts, prepublication-inconclusive.ts. File-based, write to docs/. Simple JSON write + validation.

steps.title: Create publication and delegation tools
steps.description: generate-report.ts, prepare-delegation.ts. generate-report.ts is the session report synthesizer (different from existing report.ts). File-based.

steps.title: Create enhanced read/write tools
steps.description: read-source.ts, smart-edit.ts, smart-write.ts, smart-batch.ts, replace-symbol.ts, read-lib.ts. Most complex tools. read-source.ts does Python-based source parsing (imports, exports, symbols, git dirty detection, edit metadata lookup). smart-edit.ts does edit + metadata recording. The Python scripts in these tools are substantial (100+ lines each).

steps.title: Create context curation tools
steps.description: curate-context.ts, read-artifact.ts. Session-local context assembly with archival. read-artifact.ts reads structured artifacts with profile-based filtering.

steps.title: Create rig query tools
steps.description: rig-jsonl-query.ts, rig-schema-validate.ts. rig-jsonl-query.ts is special — it needs to bypass the DuckDB firewall (which blocks read_json_auto) since querying JSONL files is its whole purpose. The .opencode version uses `uv run --with duckdb python3 -` pattern (not duckdb CLI). Consider using the CLI with read_json_auto as an unattended path.

steps.title: Update registry.ts
steps.description: Add all 27 new tool imports + registrations. Wire DuckDB write pool layer if option B was chosen. Run typecheck after this step.

steps.title: Run typecheck + tests
steps.description: `bun run typecheck` from packages/opencode/. Fix any errors. `bun test` for existing tool tests. Ensure 0 regressions.

steps.title: Final checkpoint
steps.description: Commit all new tool files, schema extension, pipeline changes, and registry updates into git.
steps.files: (all 27 new tool files + schema.duckdb.ts + pipeline.ts + registry.ts + 6 merged built-ins)

key_constraints: The DuckDB pipeline (pipeline.ts) spawns duckdb WITHOUT -readonly — this is the WRITE path. Tools that write to DuckDB must use a similar dedicated write client, NOT DuckDB.Service.
The agent-facing DuckDB.Service spawns WITH -readonly — this is the read-only path. Tools that read from DuckDB can use DuckDB.Service.

The firewall (duckdb-firewall.ts) blocks read_json_auto, read_parquet, etc. — these are blocked for agent-facing queries. The rig_jsonl_query tool needs to bypass the firewall since querying JSONL files is its whole purpose. Solutions: (a) use `uv run --with duckdb python3 -` pattern from .opencode version, (b) add a bypass parameter to the firewall, or (c) spawn duckdb for that specific tool with a bypass flag.
When OPENCODE_DUCKDB_PATH is not set, all tools fall back to JSONL — zero DuckDB dependency. No DuckDB.Service will be available in that case, and Effect.option on it yields None.
Agent profiles in .opencode/agent/ (80 .md files) stay as-is — they are loaded at runtime and do not need to be moved into source.

The old .opencode/tool/ plugin files must NOT be deleted — they serve as reference and may be needed for comparison. The migration makes them dormant (the built-in versions shadow them via the registry).
The existing registry.ts is dirty from other lanes (pre-existing indentation inconsistencies at lines 261-266). All tool additions must be additive — preserve the existing dirty code exactly.
File naming in packages/opencode/src/tool/ uses kebab-case (e.g., send-message.ts), while .opencode/tool/ uses underscores (send_message.ts). New files must use kebab-case.
The Tool.define pattern wraps the execute function with truncation and span tracking automatically. Do NOT add manual truncation or span handling in the tool body.
All new tools must return { title, metadata, output } as Tool.ExecuteResult. output must be a string.
Use Effect.orDie at the end of every execute function to convert unexpected errors into defects (the wrapping layer handles user-facing errors).
Tests cannot run from repo root (guard: do-not-run-tests-from-root). Run from packages/opencode/. Typecheck must also run from packages/opencode/.

known_risks.description: DuckDB supports 1 writer + multiple readers. Writing from multiple concurrent tools could cause lock contention. Mitigation: implement a single-writer queue (Option B approach) or use the pipeline's background fiber for all writes.

The DuckDB + JSONL pattern means two code paths per tool. Mitigation: implement JSONL as the primary path first (it's simpler and always works), then add DuckDB write as a secondary enhancement. Tests should cover both paths.

The .opencode/tool/ files embed Python scripts as string literals (50-150 lines each). Converting to built-in tools means replacing Python logic (JSON parsing, file I/O, string manipulation, date handling, SHA-256 hashing, subprocess calls) with TypeScript/Effect code. This is a substantial rewrite per tool. Expect 5-15 minutes per tool for a competent developer.
known_risks.risk_detail: Tools with the most Python code: out_of_scope_finding.ts (~103 lines Python), read_source.ts (~185 lines Python), smart_edit.ts (~90 lines Python), propose_plan.ts (~39 lines Python). These will take the longest to convert.

known_risks.description: Existing registry.ts has pre-existing modifications (indentation inconsistencies). All edits must be additive — no reformatting or cleanup of the existing code. The next session must carefully place new imports and registrations without touching adjacent code.

known_risks.risk: No tests exist for the new tools
known_risks.description: The 27 new tools have no test coverage yet. The next session should add tests for at least the file-based tools (propose-plan, revise-plan, etc.) as part of P2 work. The DuckDB-backed tools need both DuckDB and JSONL fallback test paths.

known_risks.risk: Embedded Python scripts have string escaping issues
known_risks.description: The .opencode/tool Python scripts use String.raw`...` which preserves backslashes. Converting to TypeScript means handling the escaping correctly. Pay special attention to JSON.stringify within Python code.

known_risks.risk: schema.duckdb.ts already has views, but write tables haven't been tested
known_risks.description: The existing schema.duckdb.ts only defines analytical views (CREATE VIEW), not tables (CREATE TABLE). The new write tables need CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS SQL. The pipeline needs to create these before the agent-facing DuckDB.Service tries to query them. If the pipeline hasn't run yet (first session vs reconnection), tables won't exist and queries will fail.

reference_material.opencode_tool_files: .opencode/tool/*.ts — 35 files, the source plugins to convert
reference_material.existing_builtin_tools: packages/opencode/src/tool/*.ts — existing Effect Tool.define implementations, use as reference
reference_material.tool_context: packages/opencode/src/tool/tool.ts — Tool.Context type with sessionID, messageID, agent, abort, messages, ask, metadata
reference_material.duckdb_pipeline: packages/opencode/src/storage/pipeline.ts — runPipeline, layer, write-capable duckdb subprocess spawning
reference_material.duckdb_firewall: packages/opencode/src/storage/duckdb-firewall.ts — blocked DuckDB functions for agent-facing queries
reference_material.app_filesystem: @opencode-ai/core/filesystem — Effect-powered file system service (ensureDir, writeFileString, readFileString, existsSafe)
reference_material.registry: packages/opencode/src/tool/registry.ts — tool registration pattern, layer provisioning chain
reference_material.existing_coordination_handoff: docs/json/opencode/handoffs/duckdb-pipeline-to-merge-session.v1.json — prior session handoff for DuckDB pipeline context

admonition: The .opencode/tool/ plugin files MUST NOT be deleted. They serve as the conversion source and as a fallback if the built-in conversion misses a feature. The old files become dormant once the built-in versions are registered (the registry loads built-in tools before plugin tools from directories, so built-in versions take precedence). Do NOT remove them until the built-in versions are verified to cover all functionality.

previous_handoff_history.summary: Completed DuckDB data pipeline (pipeline.ts, duckdb-config.ts, db.duckdb.ts layer, schema.duckdb.ts views). Outstanding work was to port 33 custom tools from installed OpenCode into this repo's packages/opencode/src/tool/. That port was completed by the user (35 files placed in .opencode/tool/). The current handoff supersedes the prior one for the tool conversion mission.
